hoses: Large black hard suction hose from pond floating strainer to Engine 181 intake
Attack line: 200 ft of 1¾-inch hose from Engine 181 discharge to firefighter operating on the rural grass fire
appliances: Floating strainer at end of hard suction hose
sceneElements.elevation: Level rural field operation, 0 PSI elevation correction for attack line

problems.title: Attack Line Pump Discharge Pressure
problems.question: Engine 181 is drafting from a pond and supplying one 200 ft 1¾-inch attack line with a ChiefXD 185 fog nozzle operating at 185 GPM @ 50 PSI. What pump discharge pressure should be set?
formulaBreakdown: PP = FL + NP + elevation + appliance loss.
FL = C × (GPM / 100)^2 × hose length in hundreds.
problems.instructorExplanation: The pump discharge pressure for the attack line is based on attack-line friction loss plus nozzle pressure. The hard suction, pond, and floating strainer are part of the intake side and are not added to the discharge pressure calculation.
problems.explainMistake: Do not add hard suction or drafting setup components to the attack-line pump discharge pressure. Use the attack hose friction loss plus nozzle pressure.

problems.title: Attack Line Friction Loss
problems.question: Engine 181 is supplying 185 GPM through 200 ft of 1¾-inch attack hose. What is the attack line friction loss?
formulaBreakdown: FL = C × (GPM / 100)^2 × hose length in hundreds.
Round FL to nearest 5 PSI: 105 PSI.
problems.instructorExplanation: At 185 GPM through 200 ft of 1¾-inch hose, the friction loss rounds to 105 PSI.
problems.explainMistake: Do not include nozzle pressure when the question asks only for friction loss.

problems.title: Static Lift Pressure
problems.question: If the water surface at the pond is 8 ft below the pump intake, what lift pressure must the drafting setup overcome?
formulaBreakdown: Lift pressure = lift height × 0.5 PSI per foot.
problems.instructorExplanation: The drafting setup must overcome the vertical lift from the water surface to the pump intake. At 8 ft of lift, the pressure equivalent is 4 PSI.
problems.explainMistake: Do not calculate lift from the length of the hard suction hose. Lift is based on vertical distance from the water surface to the pump intake.

problems.title: Static Water Duration
problems.question: If the accessible static water supply contains 12000 usable gallons and Engine 181 is flowing 185 GPM, how long will the water supply last?
problems.instructorExplanation: Water supply duration is the usable water volume divided by the operating flow. At 185 GPM, 12000 gallons lasts about 65 minutes.
problems.explainMistake: Do not use pump discharge pressure to calculate water duration. Duration is based only on available gallons and flow.

problems.title: Two-Line Drafting Water Duration
problems.question: Engine 181 is drafting from a pond with 12,000 usable gallons available. If two identical ChiefXD 185 attack lines are placed in service, each flowing 185 GPM, about how many minutes can the static water supply support the operation?
formulaBreakdown: Operating time = usable gallons ÷ total GPM.
problems.instructorExplanation: Static-water duration problems require the operator to total all operating flows, then divide the usable water supply by that total flow. Two 185 GPM lines use 370 GPM together.
problems.explainMistake: Do not use only one line when two lines are operating. The water supply duration is based on total flow from the static water source.
